title: 2.3 Structure and Function of Plant Parts
description: Structure and function of plant parts — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: What are the two major systems of the external structure of a typical angiosperm?
options: The root system and the flower system
The shoot system and the root system
The leaf system and the stem system
The vascular system and the reproductive system
questions.correctAnswer: The shoot system and the root system

questions.question: What does the shoot system of a plant include?
options: Stem, branches, leaves, buds, flowers and fruits
questions.correctAnswer: Stem, branches, leaves, buds, flowers and fruits

questions.question: Where is the shoot system usually found?
What organs may be missing from the shoot system depending on the reproductive stage?
What does the root system include?
options: Primary or tap root, lateral or branch roots, root hairs and root cap
questions.correctAnswer: Primary or tap root, lateral or branch roots, root hairs and root cap

questions.question: How are roots distinguished from underground stems?
options: Roots do not bear either leaves or buds
questions.correctAnswer: Roots do not bear either leaves or buds

questions.question: What are the external parts of a typical leaf?
options: Petiole, lamina, midrib, margin, base and tips
questions.correctAnswer: Petiole, lamina, midrib, margin, base and tips

questions.question: Which part of the leaf is described as the broadest, flat, wide and commonly thin?
What does the broad and flat lamina provide for the leaf?
options: Large surface area to collect light
questions.correctAnswer: Large surface area to collect light

questions.question: What does the thinness of the lamina create?
options: Short distance for gas exchange through stomata
questions.correctAnswer: Short distance for gas exchange through stomata

questions.question: What does the midrib of a leaf contain?
options: The vein (transporting vessels) and supportive tissues
questions.correctAnswer: The vein (transporting vessels) and supportive tissues

questions.question: What are the two basic types of roots?
What is a characteristic of a tap-root system?
options: Many thin roots of similar size
One large primary vertical root with few lateral roots
Roots that spread near the surface
questions.correctAnswer: One large primary vertical root with few lateral roots

questions.question: How does a tap-root provide stability?
options: By penetrating deep into the soil
questions.correctAnswer: By penetrating deep into the soil

questions.question: Which group of plants typically has a tap-root system?
How is a fibrous root formed?
options: By thin moderately branching roots growing from stems
questions.correctAnswer: By thin moderately branching roots growing from stems

questions.question: What are fibrous roots efficient at?
options: Absorbing water and minerals close to the soil surface
questions.correctAnswer: Absorbing water and minerals close to the soil surface

questions.question: What ecological benefit do fibrous roots provide?
options: They prevent soil erosion by holding soil together
They add nutrients to the soil
questions.correctAnswer: They prevent soil erosion by holding soil together

questions.question: Which group of plants typically has fibrous roots?
What is the outer layer of a leaf called?
What covers the upper epidermis of a leaf?
What is the function of the waxy cuticle on the upper epidermis?
What type of cells are found in the lower epidermis that leave open spaces?
What are the open spaces in the lower epidermis called?
What functions do stomata regulate?
options: Oxygen release, carbon dioxide intake and water loss
questions.correctAnswer: Oxygen release, carbon dioxide intake and water loss

questions.question: Why are stomata more abundant in the lower epidermis of most leaves?
options: To reduce water loss due to direct sunlight
questions.correctAnswer: To reduce water loss due to direct sunlight

questions.question: What is the middle layer of a leaf called?
What are the two regions of the mesophyll layer?
How are palisade cells arranged?
options: Regularly arranged and closely packed columnar cells
In a single layer of flat cells
questions.correctAnswer: Regularly arranged and closely packed columnar cells

questions.question: Which layer of the leaf contains the largest number of chloroplasts per cell?
Why is the palisade layer best positioned for capturing sunlight?
options: It is near the lower epidermis
It is immediately beneath the upper epidermis
It is in the center of the leaf
It has the most air spaces
questions.correctAnswer: It is immediately beneath the upper epidermis

questions.question: What do the slight separations between columnar cells in the palisade layer maximize?
options: Diffusion of carbon dioxide and capillary movement of water
questions.correctAnswer: Diffusion of carbon dioxide and capillary movement of water

questions.question: How are spongy layer cells characterized?
options: Irregularly shaped with fewer chloroplasts and numerous air spaces
Closely packed with no air spaces
questions.correctAnswer: Irregularly shaped with fewer chloroplasts and numerous air spaces

questions.question: What do the air spaces in the spongy layer allow?
options: Diffusion of oxygen, water vapour and carbon dioxide
questions.correctAnswer: Diffusion of oxygen, water vapour and carbon dioxide

questions.question: What is the outermost layer of a dicot stem called?
What is the outer walls of the stem epidermis thickened with to minimize transpiration?
What do the compactly arranged cells of the stem epidermis protect against?
options: Mechanical injury and entry of harmful organisms
questions.correctAnswer: Mechanical injury and entry of harmful organisms

questions.question: What lies below the epidermis in a dicot stem?
What type of cells is the hypodermis mainly composed of?
What gives the hypodermis its ability to provide mechanical strength?
options: Thickening at the corners due to cellulose deposition
questions.correctAnswer: Thickening at the corners due to cellulose deposition

questions.question: What is the cortex in a dicot stem composed of?
options: Thick-walled cells with no intercellular space
Thin-walled large round or oval cells with intercellular space
questions.correctAnswer: Thin-walled large round or oval cells with intercellular space

questions.question: What is the function of the cortex in a dicot stem?
What is the endodermis in a stem?
options: The outermost layer of the stem
The innermost layer of the cortex separating cortex from vascular bundles
The central part of the stem
questions.correctAnswer: The innermost layer of the cortex separating cortex from vascular bundles

questions.question: What do endodermis cells usually contain?
What is the endodermis sometimes called due to its starch content?
What are vascular bundles in a stem composed of?
options: Xylem and phloem arranged in a ring around the pith
questions.correctAnswer: Xylem and phloem arranged in a ring around the pith

questions.question: Where is xylem located within each vascular bundle of a dicot stem?
options: In the center of the bundle

questions.question: Where is phloem located within each vascular bundle of a dicot stem?
What is found between xylem and phloem in a vascular bundle?
options: A strip of actively dividing young cells (cambium)
questions.correctAnswer: A strip of actively dividing young cells (cambium)

questions.question: What does xylem transport?
What does phloem transport?
What occupies the central portion of a dicot stem?
What are the cells of the pith composed of?
options: Thin-walled cells that are rounded or polygonal
questions.correctAnswer: Thin-walled cells that are rounded or polygonal

questions.question: What functions does the pith serve?
options: Food storage and help in internal translocation of water
questions.correctAnswer: Food storage and help in internal translocation of water

questions.question: How can angiosperms be classified based on cotyledon number?
How many sets of vascular bundles does a dicot stem have?
options: Few (4 to 8) sets of vascular bundles
questions.correctAnswer: Few (4 to 8) sets of vascular bundles

questions.question: How are vascular bundles arranged in a dicot stem?
options: Arranged in the form of one or two rings
questions.correctAnswer: Arranged in the form of one or two rings

questions.question: What is the pith like in a dicot stem?
What is the outermost layer of a dicot root called?
What does the piliferous layer consist of?
What is absent in the piliferous layer?
What type of cells make up the cortex of a dicot root?
options: Thin-walled oval or rounded loosely arranged cells with intercellular spaces
questions.correctAnswer: Thin-walled oval or rounded loosely arranged cells with intercellular spaces

questions.question: What does the cortex of a root store?
What is the endodermis in a root made of?
What does the endodermis layer help with?
options: Movement of water and dissolved nutrients from cortex into xylem
questions.correctAnswer: Movement of water and dissolved nutrients from cortex into xylem

questions.question: What is the pericycle in a root?
options: A single layer inner to endodermis that is the site of origin of lateral roots
questions.correctAnswer: A single layer inner to endodermis that is the site of origin of lateral roots

questions.question: Where do lateral roots originate?
What do the vascular bundles in a root consist of?
options: Xylem and phloem with cambium between them
questions.correctAnswer: Xylem and phloem with cambium between them

questions.question: How is the pith described in dicot roots?
options: Present in young roots while absent in old roots
questions.correctAnswer: Present in young roots while absent in old roots

questions.question: How is the cortex area in a dicot root described?
How many vascular bundles does a dicot root typically have?
What describes the pith in a dicot root?
Do dicot roots show secondary growth?
options: No, they never show secondary growth
Yes, due to the presence of cambium
questions.correctAnswer: Yes, due to the presence of cambium
